ogTitle: Shark Creek vs Sheedys Gully: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Shark Creek and Sheedys Gully.
heading: Shark Creek vs Sheedys Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Shark Creek, New South Wales and Sheedys Gully, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Shark Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Sheedys Gully (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Shark Creek or Sheedys Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Shark Creek has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Sheedys Gully scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Shark Creek scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Sheedys Gully scores 63/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Shark Creek or Sheedys Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Shark Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
